原文: I have a drawer full of letters to Santa. Each year my children would write up their Christmas wish lists. They were polite requests for the latest game or toy. As the children got older, they would give Santa some hints(提示) on where to buy their presents. D. As a result, each year they received something they wanted.
翻译: 我有一个抽屉，里面装满了写给圣诞老人的信。每年我的孩子们都会写下他们的圣诞愿望清单。这些都是对最新游戏或玩具的礼貌请求。随着孩子们长大，他们会给圣诞老人一些关于在哪里购买礼物的提示。因此，每年他们都能收到他们想要的东西。

① drawer /drɔː(r)/ n. 抽屉
Ⓡ draw /drɔː/ v. 画；拉
Ⓡ drawing /ˈdrɔːɪŋ/ n. 绘画
Ⓡ withdraw /wɪðˈdrɔː/ v. 撤回；取款
Ⓡ withdrawal /wɪðˈdrɔːəl/ n. 撤回；取款
② polite /pəˈlaɪt/ adj. 礼貌的
Ⓡ impolite /ˌɪmpəˈlaɪt/ adj. 不礼貌的
Ⓡ politely /pəˈlaɪtli/ adv. 礼貌地
Ⓡ politeness /pəˈlaɪtnəs/ n. 礼貌
③ request /rɪˈkwest/ n. 请求 v. 请求
Ⓡ require /rɪˈkwaɪə(r)/ v. 需要；要求
Ⓡ requirement /rɪˈkwaɪəmənt/ n. 要求；必要条件
Ⓡ inquire /ɪnˈkwaɪə(r)/ v. 询问
④ latest /ˈleɪtɪst/ adj. 最新的
Ⓡ late /leɪt/ adj. 晚的；迟到的
Ⓡ later /ˈleɪtə(r)/ adv. 后来
Ⓡ lately /ˈleɪtli/ adv. 最近
⑤ hint /hɪnt/ n. 提示 v. 暗示
Ⓡ clue /kluː/ n. 线索
Ⓡ suggest /səˈdʒest/ v. 建议；暗示
Ⓡ suggestion /səˈdʒestʃən/ n. 建议；暗示

原文: Those letters taught my children a valuable lesson about writing: It has a purpose. Sometimes our purpose is to achieve something; other times it may be to entertain, inform, or persuade. B. But it is always purposeful—or at least it should be. And when writing doesn't seem to have any purpose or get any kind of meaningful response, then it can be hard to get excited about doing it.
翻译: 那些信件教会了我的孩子们关于写作的宝贵一课：写作是有目的的。有时我们的目的是为了达成某事；其他时候可能是为了娱乐、告知或说服。但它总是有目的的——或者至少应该是。当写作似乎没有任何目的或得不到任何有意义的回应时，就很难对它感到兴奋。
必背词汇:
⑥ valuable /ˈvæljuəbl/ adj. 宝贵的；有价值的
Ⓡ value /ˈvæljuː/ n. 价值 v. 重视
Ⓡ invaluable /ɪnˈvæljuəbl/ adj. 无价的
Ⓡ evaluate /ɪˈvæljueɪt/ v. 评估
⑦ purpose /ˈpɜːpəs/ n. 目的
Ⓡ purposeful /ˈpɜːpəsfəl/ adj. 有目的的
Ⓡ purposely /ˈpɜːpəsli/ adv. 故意地
Ⓡ on purpose 故意地
⑧ achieve /əˈtʃiːv/ v. 实现；达到
Ⓡ achievement /əˈtʃiːvmənt/ n. 成就
Ⓡ achievable /əˈtʃiːvəbl/ adj. 可实现的
Ⓡ reach /riːtʃ/ v. 达到；到达
⑨ entertain /ˌentəˈteɪn/ v. 娱乐；招待

Ⓡ amuse /əˈmjuːz/ v. 使娱乐
⑩ inform /ɪnˈfɔːm/ v. 告知；通知
Ⓡ information /ˌɪnfəˈmeɪʃn/ n. 信息
Ⓡ informative /ɪnˈfɔːmətɪv/ adj. 提供信息的
Ⓡ formal /ˈfɔːml/ adj. 正式的
⑪ persuade /pəˈsweɪd/ v. 说服；劝说
Ⓡ persuasion /pəˈsweɪʒn/ n. 说服
Ⓡ persuasive /pəˈsweɪsɪv/ adj. 有说服力的
Ⓡ convince /kənˈvɪns/ v. 使确信
⑫ meaningful /ˈmiːnɪŋfl/ adj. 有意义的
Ⓡ meaning /ˈmiːnɪŋ/ n. 意义
Ⓡ meaningless /ˈmiːnɪŋləs/ adj. 无意义的
Ⓡ significance /sɪɡˈnɪfɪkəns/ n. 重要性

原文: Christmas is a great time of year for children to communicate with others through writing. When children write for real audiences and real purposes, they can learn to choose their words accordingly. E. You write "Hi Nanna and Pop" because you are close to them. You write "Dear Santa" because you don't know him personally and you want to show him respect.
翻译: 圣诞节是一年中孩子们通过写作与他人交流的好时机。当孩子们为真实的受众和真实的目的写作时，他们就能学会相应地选择用词。你写“嗨，奶奶和爷爷”是因为你与他们亲近。你写“亲爱的圣诞老人”是因为你个人不认识他，你想表示对他的尊重。
必背词汇:
⑬ communicate /kəˈmjuːnɪkeɪt/ v. 交流；沟通
Ⓡ communication /kəˌmjuːnɪˈkeɪʃn/ n. 交流；通讯
Ⓡ communicative /kəˈmjuːnɪkətɪv/ adj. 健谈的
Ⓡ community /kəˈmjuːnəti/ n. 社区
⑭ audience /ˈɔːdiəns/ n. 观众；听众；读者
Ⓡ audio /ˈɔːdiəʊ/ adj. 声音的
Ⓡ auditorium /ˌɔːdɪˈtɔːriəm/ n. 礼堂
Ⓡ audition /ɔːˈdɪʃn/ n. 试镜
⑮ accordingly /əˈkɔːdɪŋli/ adv. 相应地；因此
Ⓡ according to 根据
Ⓡ accord /əˈkɔːd/ n. 一致 v. 一致
Ⓡ record /ˈrekɔːd/ n. 记录 /rɪˈkɔːd/ v. 记录
⑯ personally /ˈpɜːsənəli/ adv. 个人地；亲自地
Ⓡ personal /ˈpɜːsənl/ adj. 个人的
Ⓡ personality /ˌpɜːsəˈnæləti/ n. 个性
Ⓡ personnel /ˌpɜːsəˈnel/ n. 人员
⑰ respect /rɪˈspekt/ n. 尊重 v. 尊重
Ⓡ respectful /rɪˈspektfl/ adj. 表示尊敬的
Ⓡ respectable /rɪˈspektəbl/ adj. 值得尊敬的
Ⓡ respective /rɪˈspektɪv/ adj. 各自的

原文: F. As technology develops, young people are writing more than they ever have. Texting, online messaging, live chats while playing video games all mean that they are constantly producing written messages. However, because they are usually writing to family and friends, they use informal language most of the time.
翻译: 随着技术的发展，年轻人写作比以往任何时候都多。发短信、在线消息、玩电子游戏时的实时聊天都意味着他们不断产生书面信息。然而，因为他们通常是写给家人和朋友，所以大多数时候使用非正式语言。
必背词汇:
⑱ technology /tekˈnɒlədʒi/ n. 技术
Ⓡ technical /ˈteknɪkl/ adj. 技术的
Ⓡ technician /tekˈnɪʃn/ n. 技术员
Ⓡ technique /tekˈniːk/ n. 技巧
⑲ constantly /ˈkɒnstəntli/ adv. 不断地；经常地
Ⓡ constant /ˈkɒnstənt/ adj. 持续的；不变的
Ⓡ constantly changing 不断变化的
Ⓡ frequently /ˈfriːkwəntli/ adv. 频繁地
⑳ informal /ɪnˈfɔːml/ adj. 非正式的
Ⓡ formal /ˈfɔːml/ adj. 正式的
Ⓡ informality /ˌɪnfɔːˈmæləti/ n. 非正式
Ⓡ casually /ˈkæʒuəli/ adv. 随意地

原文: There is nothing wrong with this kind of writing. It suits the audience and the purpose and gets the job done. G. But if this is the only kind of writing young people do, it will cause problems. This type of writing won't be rewarded by the school examiner, nor by a future employer or a potential client. The very best communicators are those who know how to adjust their language to match their audience.
翻译: 这种写作没有什么错。它适合受众和目的，并能完成任务。但如果这是年轻人做的唯一一种写作，就会导致问题。这种写作不会得到学校考官、未来雇主或潜在客户的奖励。最好的沟通者是那些知道如何调整语言以适应受众的人。
必背词汇:
㉑ suit /suːt/ v. 适合；相配 n. 套装

Ⓡ suite /swiːt/ n. 套房；一套

㉕ potential /pəˈtenʃl/ adj. 潜在的 n. 潜力
